title: Marlierea guildingiana

This family consists of around 145 genera and over 6,000 species, many of which are native to Australia and the surrounding islands. Marlierea guildingiana is further classified into the genus Marlierea, which includes about 60 species of flowering plants. These plants are primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions, particularly in Central and South America. The classification of Marlierea guildingiana reflects its evolutionary relationships with other species within the Myrtaceae family.
